title: Tracked FortiGate config backup exposes encrypted private keys and credential material
sink_or_control: git repository artifact accessible to anyone with repo access
evidence: grep/nl review found FortiGate `set api-key ENC`, multiple `set password ENC` entries, multiple `-----BEGIN ENCRYPTED PRIVATE KEY-----` blocks, `set save-password enable`, and `set psksecret ENC`. `git check-ignore -v` showed `ansible/artifacts/` is ignored, but `git ls-files` still lists the artifact, proving it is tracked despite the ignore rule.
candidate_local_validation: Validated from current worktree line-numbered reads and tracked-file checks.
attack_path_facts: A repo reader or fork recipient can obtain the complete firewall backup, attempt offline recovery/decryption of FortiGate ENC material, reuse private key material if passphrases are weak or available elsewhere, and learn sensitive VPN/certificate/admin configuration. Even encrypted secrets should not be committed because revocation/rotation becomes necessary after disclosure.
